No evidence suggests employees are unwilling to work.
Most delays appear to occur while waiting for decisions rather than performing work.

theories.title: Centralized governance bottleneck
theories.explanation: Decision authority appears concentrated in ways that slow execution, reduce local ownership, or create dependency on a small number of leaders.

semanticConcepts.statement: Leadership-facing evidence suggests confidence, but other signals point to operational or customer-side constraints. Discovery currently believes the narrative may be stronger than the underlying evidence. However, Discovery also found a tension: Leadership confidence conflicts with customer friction.
